translation: [All] praise is [due] to Allah, Creator of the heavens and the earth, [who] made the angels messengers having wings, two or three or four. He increases in creation what He wills. Indeed, Allah is over all things competent
commentary:
ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فَاطِرِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ جَاعِلِ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ رُسُلًا أُو۟لِىٓ أَجْنِحَةٍ مَّثْنَىٰ وَثُلَـٰثَ وَرُبَـٰعَ ۚ يَزِيدُ فِى ٱلْخَلْقِ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ

All praise and gratitude belongs to Allah , فَاطِرِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - the Carver, the creative Designer and Creator of the heavens and the earth. After establishing this foundation of His creative power over the visible universe, Allah ﷻ introduces us to an aspect of His creation that lies beyond our direct experience: جَاعِلِ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ رُسُلًا - the Maker of angels into messengers.

The ayah then describes these angelic messengers as possessing wings: أُو۟لِىٓ أَجْنِحَةٍ مَّثْنَىٰ وَثُلَـٰثَ وَرُبَـٰعَ - some with two wings each, some with three, and some with four. This variation in their design reflects the different roles and responsibilities assigned to different species of angels. The distance they must travel and the magnitude of their missions determine the number of wings they are given. According to one narration, angel Jibreel (AS) has been described as having 70,000 wings, reflecting the immense scope of his responsibilities as the carrier of divine revelation.

The ayah continues: يَزِيدُ فِى ٱلْخَلْقِ مَا يَشَآءُ - He increases in His creation whatever He wills. This speaks to the infinite diversity and complexity possible within Allah's creation. Finally, the ayah concludes with a powerful declaration: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ - certainly Allah is in complete control over all things. This closing statement reminds us that the same Power that created the heavens, earth and angels is capable of anything He wills.

translation: Whatever Allah grants to people of mercy - none can withhold it; and whatever He withholds - none can release it thereafter. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise
commentary:
مَّا يَفْتَحِ ٱللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِن رَّحْمَةٍ فَلَا مُمْسِكَ لَهَا ۖ وَمَا يُمْسِكْ فَلَا مُرْسِلَ لَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ

This ayah establishes a foundational principle about Allah's control over mercy and blessing. مَّا يَفْتَحِ ٱللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِن رَّحْمَةٍ - whatever doors of mercy Allah ﷻ opens for people, فَلَا مُمْسِكَ لَهَا - there is absolutely no one who can prevent those mercies from flowing or close those doors. Conversely, وَمَا يُمْسِكْ - whatever He might close off or withhold, فَلَا مُرْسِلَ لَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ - there is no one who can send it forth or open it after Him.

This principle has been understood by some scholars to apply directly to divine revelation itself. Now that Allah ﷻ has opened the door to revelation through the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, no force in creation can close it. And once Allah ﷻ sealed revelation after the Prophet's passing, no one will be able to open that door again until the Day of Judgment.

The ayah concludes: وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ - and He is the Ultimate Authority, the All-Wise. These two divine attributes together explain why His decisions about opening and closing doors of mercy are absolute: He has complete authority to do so (العزيز), and His decisions are always grounded in perfect wisdom (الحكيم).

translation: O mankind, remember the favor of Allah upon you. Is there any creator other than Allah who provides for you from the heaven and earth? There is no deity except Him, so how are you deluded
commentary:
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ هَلْ مِنْ خَـٰلِقٍ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ

The address يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ - O people! - is a call to all of humanity, a command that demands attention. ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ - make mention of the favor of Allah upon you, remember and acknowledge His blessings.

Then comes a rhetorical question that forces reflection: هَلْ مِنْ خَـٰلِقٍ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - Is there any creator other than Allah at all who provides for you from the heavens and the earth? Allah ﷻ provides the rain from the sky and all forms of sustenance from the earth. The question is designed to make the answer obvious: there is no other provider, no other sustainer.

Therefore: لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ - no one deserves to be worshiped and obeyed except He alone. The logical conclusion follows: فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ - then how in the world are you being turned away? How are you diverted to worship anything or anyone else when the evidence of His exclusive right to worship is so clear?

translation: And if they deny you, [O Muhammad] - already were messengers denied before you. And to Allah are returned [all] matters
commentary:
وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ ۚ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ

This ayah provides consolation to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and establishes a pattern across prophetic history. وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ - if they deny you and call you a liar, فَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ - then messengers before you were also denied. This is not new. Rejection of divine guidance has been the pattern of many people throughout history.

However, the ayah ends with a reminder of ultimate accountability: وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ - and to Allah all matters will be returned in the end. Those who reject today will face the consequences of their rejection. Those who accepted truth and suffered for it will receive their justice. Every decision, every stance taken in this life will return to Allah ﷻ for final judgment.

translation: O mankind, indeed the promise of Allah is truth, so let not the worldly life delude you and be not deceived about Allah by the Deceiver
commentary:
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۖ فَلَا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَا يَغُرَّنَّكُم بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْغَرُورُ

Once again, the universal call: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ - O people! This address is for everyone, but will be heard primarily by Muslims since they are the ones receiving the Quran. إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ - indeed, the promise of Allah is absolute truth. Pay attention to this reality.

Then comes a critical warning: فَلَا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا - so do not let worldly life deceive you. The word غَرَّ in Arabic means to be conned, to be tricked, to be playing along with something only to discover at the very end that it was all a deception.

Worldly life can be captivating in countless ways. People develop different obsessions: some become workaholics, others are consumed by sports, entertainment, food, fashion, beauty, status or fame. Each person has their own particular attachment to this temporary world. Then there are those who simply want to drift through life without purpose or direction, with no greater ambition than to "chill" today, tomorrow and forever, with no plans, no goals, heading towards nothing greater.

This warning applies even to Muslims. When a believer becomes immersed in worldly pursuits, Shaytan modifies his approach. His deceptions for people of shirk are different from his deceptions for Muslims. 

وَلَا يَغُرَّنَّكُم بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْغَرُورُ - and do not let the ultimate deceiver (Shaytan) deceive you in regards to Allah. For Muslims, Shaytan's strategy shifts. He whispers: "Sure, you've messed up, but you're not that bad. You prayed Fajr twice this week, didn't you? You fasted Ramadan. So what if you engage in a few questionable things? Your good deeds should compensate."

This is one of Shaytan's primary tricks: making you proud of your good deeds while convincing you they should cancel out your bad behavior. He suggests your occasional prayers or yearly Hajj plans should be sufficient, regardless of how you earn your money or what sins you regularly commit.

Another common deception: "Allah is so merciful! His mercy is infinite. Why would He ever punish you? Your crimes are so small compared to His vast mercy." This misunderstanding of divine mercy ignores divine justice.

A third trap: Shaytan speaks to you about Allah ﷻ Himself, distorting your understanding of what He expects from you. "Allah gave you this talent, these good looks - why wouldn't you use them however you want? That's why He gave them to you!" 

There are Muslim entertainers who consider themselves spiritual, claiming their music or acting is what Allah ﷻ wants from them. Some become ambassadors of Muslim humiliation in the entertainment industry, then donate to humanitarian causes during Ramadan, as if that compensates for everything else. Good deeds are indeed good, but they don't automatically cancel out persistent wrongdoing. Our deen doesn't work that way.

translation: Indeed, Satan is an enemy to you; so take him as an enemy. He only invites his party to be among the companions of the Blaze
commentary:
إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ فَٱتَّخِذُوهُ عَدُوًّا ۚ إِنَّمَا يَدْعُوا۟ حِزْبَهُۥ لِيَكُونُوا۟ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ

This ayah makes Shaytan's status crystal clear: إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ - indeed, Shaytan is an enemy to you. This is not an opinion or a theory; it's a statement of fact. فَٱتَّخِذُوهُ عَدُوًّا - therefore, you must take him as an enemy. Treat him as the enemy he is.

Why this emphasis? إِنَّمَا يَدْعُوا۟ حِزْبَهُۥ لِيَكُونُوا۟ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ - he only calls his party, his followers, his حِزْب, so that they may become companions of the blazing Fire. Whoever listens to Shaytan, whoever takes influence from him becomes part of his party. His goal for his followers is not success or happiness - it's the Fire. That's his only objective for those who follow him.

translation: Those who disbelieve will have a severe punishment, and those who believe and do righteous deeds will have forgiveness and great reward
commentary:
ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ

This ayah presents the ultimate contrast in destinies. ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ - those who disbelieved, for them is severe punishment. Their rejection of truth leads to one outcome.

But وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ - those who believed and did the righteous deeds that were expected of them, لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ - especially for them, and them alone, will be forgiveness and a huge compensation. Notice the pairing: belief must be accompanied by righteous action. Faith and deeds together constitute the path to divine forgiveness and immense reward.

translation: Then is one to whom the evil of his deed has been made attractive so he considers it good [like one rightly guided]? For indeed, Allah sends astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. So do not let yourself perish over them in regret. Indeed, Allah is Knowing of what they do
commentary:
أَفَمَن زُيِّنَ لَهُۥ سُوٓءُ عَمَلِهِۦ فَرَءَاهُ حَسَنًا ۖ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُضِلُّ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ فَلَا تَذْهَبْ نَفْسُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسَرَٰتٍ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَصْنَعُونَ

This ayah addresses a disturbing spiritual reality: أَفَمَن زُيِّنَ لَهُۥ سُوٓءُ عَمَلِهِۦ فَرَءَاهُ حَسَنًا - what about the one for whom his evil deeds have been beautified, so he sees them as good? This person doesn't just commit wrong - he loves doing wrong, he's obsessed with it, he glorifies it.

Look at our societies. Consider recent controversies where people demonstrate publicly for causes that Islam considers immoral, acts of فَاحِشَة (gross indecency). In their minds, they're working for humanitarian causes, for justice, for progress. They believe they're liberating humanity from "close-minded religious thought."

Throughout history, people work towards causes that sound noble on the surface - women's liberation, equal rights - but when women are objectified as mere flesh in the advertising industry, the same voices remain silent. Why? Because it's given a pretty title: "women's freedom to choose."

This is the pattern: give evil a beautiful name and people will accept it. During the Vietnam War, transparent journalism showing images of villages destroyed, children killed and civilians harmed caused major protests in the United States. There was popular uprising against the war. But how many such images from Iraq or Afghanistan have been aired? The stories focus on military casualties, but civilian suffering is hidden. Why? To beautify the narrative: "freedom for Iraq," "liberation of these lands."

This is what all oppressors do - they spin their most evil deeds with good names like "freedom" or "civil rights." It's not unique to any one nation. Consider the Iran-Iraq war, where both sides called their actions jihad fi sabilillah. What a beautiful, noble term from the Quran, yet it was used to justify chemical weapons and horrific violence on both sides.

The ayah continues: فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُضِلُّ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ - indeed, Allah allows to go astray whomever He wills, and He guides whomever He wills. When people persistently choose falsehood over truth, Allah ﷻ allows them to continue on their path of misguidance.

Then comes crucial counsel to the Prophet ﷺ: فَلَا تَذْهَبْ نَفْسُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسَرَٰتٍ - so do not destroy yourself in regrets over them, don't let yourself be consumed by grief upon grief over them. 

This guidance extends to all believers. Yes, there is frustrating news in world politics and events. Propaganda against Muslims continues, the good of the Quran is portrayed as evil while actual evil is beautified - it's all deeply frustrating. But we shouldn't become so overwhelmed that we can do nothing else. We shouldn't spend all our energy just venting anger at the news.

Speaking out has its place, but if it's just hot air with no concrete plan of action afterward, then what have we accomplished? The Prophet ﷺ was on a mission. Problems arose constantly, but he didn't become paralyzed by them. He stayed on course, remained focused on his objectives. This is the sense of direction the Ummah needs from its scholars and leaders today.

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَصْنَعُونَ - indeed Allah is fully knowledgeable of what they manufacture, what they do. He knows their deeds and their intentions, and He will hold them accountable.

translation: And it is Allah who sends the winds, and they stir the clouds, and We drive them to a dead land and give life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness. Thus is the resurrection
commentary:
وَٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَرْسَلَ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًا فَسُقْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰ بَلَدٍ مَّيِّتٍ فَأَحْيَيْنَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلنُّشُورُ

After discussing spiritual life and death, the ayah presents a powerful metaphor from the natural world. وَٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَرْسَلَ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ - Allah is the One who sends the winds, فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًا - and they stir up clouds, gathering them together.

فَسُقْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰ بَلَدٍ مَّيِّتٍ - then We drive those clouds to a land that was dead, barren, lifeless. فَأَحْيَيْنَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا - then We give life to that land after it had died. The rain falls, and suddenly the dead earth bursts forth with vegetation, with life, with color.

كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلنُّشُورُ - and just like that, exactly like that, will be the resurrection. Just as dead land comes back to life with rain, so will the dead be brought back to life on the Day of Judgment. The One who can revive dead earth can certainly revive dead humans. This natural phenomenon we witness repeatedly serves as a sign and proof of the resurrection to come.

translation: Whoever desires honor [through power] - then to Allah belongs all honor. To Him ascends good speech, and righteous work raises it. But they who plot evil deeds will have a severe punishment, and the plotting of those - it will perish
commentary:
مَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ ٱلْعِزَّةَ فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ ٱلْكَلِمُ ٱلطَّيِّبُ وَٱلْعَمَلُ ٱلصَّـٰلِحُ يَرْفَعُهُۥ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ يَمْكُرُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ ۖ وَمَكْرُ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُوَ يَبُورُ

This ayah begins with a profound statement about the source of all honor: مَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ ٱلْعِزَّةَ - whoever seeks honor, dignity, respect, authority, فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعًا - then let them know that to Allah alone belongs all honor, all dignity, all respect, all authority. If you want real عِزَّة, you must seek it from its only Source.

Then comes a beautiful description of how our deeds rise to Allah إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ ٱلْكَلِمُ ٱلطَّيِّبُ - to Him ascends the good word. The verb يَصْعَدُ means to climb, like climbing a mountain - a slow, difficult ascent where one might slip or fall. When we speak good words to one another, when we share advice, when we recite the word of Allah, when we make dua for someone - these good words are carried by angels and they climb toward Allah ﷻ.

But the climbing imagery shows something important: the word by itself is rising, it's heading toward Allah ﷻ, but it's struggling, making slow progress. So what gives it a push? وَٱلْعَمَلُ ٱلصَّـٰلِحُ يَرْفَعُهُۥ - and righteous action lifts it up, elevates it, gives it momentum.

This is a crucial teaching: good speech is valuable, it's heading in the right direction toward acceptance and reward. But if you really want to expedite that ascent, if you want your words to reach Allah ﷻ with force, then follow them up with action. Words paired with deeds rise powerfully toward divine acceptance.

The ayah then contrasts this with another group: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَمْكُرُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ - as for those who are plotting and scheming their evil plans, لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ - they will have severe punishment. وَمَكْرُ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُوَ يَبُورُ - and the plotting and scheming of such people will slowly deteriorate until it's bankrupt. The word يَبُورُ indicates gradual decline, reducing down and down until the scheme is reduced to rubble, to nothing. While good deeds ascend, evil schemes descend into ruin.

translation: And Allah created you from dust, then from a sperm-drop; then He made you mates. And no female conceives nor does she give birth except with His knowledge. And no aged person is granted [additional] life nor is his lifespan lessened but that it is in a register. Indeed, that for Allah is easy
commentary:
وَٱللَّهُ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا ۚ وَمَا تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنثَىٰ وَلَا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِۦ ۚ وَمَا يُعَمَّرُ مِن مُّعَمَّرٍ وَلَا يُنقَصُ مِنْ عُمُرِهِۦٓ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ

This ayah takes us back to the beginning of human creation and Allah's complete knowledge of every stage. وَٱللَّهُ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ - Allah created you from dust, from the earth. ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ - then from a fluid, a drop. ثُمَّ جَعَلَكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا - then He made you into pairs, spouses, males and females.

Now comes an extraordinary statement of divine knowledge: وَمَا تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنثَىٰ وَلَا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِۦ - there is not a single female creature on earth that carries anything in her womb, nor delivers, except that He knows all about it. Every pregnancy, every birth across all of creation is fully known to Allah ﷻ.

Consider how much parents wonder about their unborn children. They go to ultrasounds to learn if it's a boy or girl. Then they talk to each other: "I wonder what he'll be like? Tall or short? Will he look like you or me?" When the child is five, they're still wondering: "What will this kid do when he grows up?" At ten: "Can this child do anything?" At eighteen or nineteen: "What will your future be?" Questions, questions, questions about their children's futures.

But Allah ﷻ says: I knew all about this child before he was in his mother's belly, when she was carrying him, when she delivered him, and everything that will come after.

وَمَا يُعَمَّرُ مِن مُّعَمَّرٍ - and whoever is given old age, whoever reaches their eighties, nineties, or even one hundred years, وَلَا يُنقَصُ مِنْ عُمُرِهِۦٓ - and whoever is reduced from the average lifespan, whoever dies younger than expected - people say things like "he was taken too soon" or "she died ahead of her time."

إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ - but all of this is recorded in a Book. It was already written: this child will live seven years, this one seventy-seven years, this one twenty-seven years. Visit a Muslim graveyard and walk among the graves, reading the dates on the headstones. You'll see 1922-2002, or 1965-1992, or 2002-2003. SubhanAllah, some lives are long, some are short, but all are exactly as Allah ﷻ decided and recorded.

إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ - indeed, all of that is easy for Allah. Recording every life, knowing every detail, determining every span - none of this is difficult for Him. It's all يَسِيرٌ - completely easy for the One who created us from dust.

translation: And not alike are the two bodies of water. One is fresh and sweet, palatable for drinking, and one is salty and bitter. And from each you eat tender meat and extract ornaments which you wear, and you see the ships plowing through [them] that you might seek of His bounty; and perhaps you will be grateful
commentary:
وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْبَحْرَانِ هَـٰذَا عَذْبٌ فُرَاتٌ سَآئِغٌ شَرَابُهُۥ وَهَـٰذَا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌ ۖ وَمِن كُلٍّ تَأْكُلُونَ لَحْمًا طَرِيًّا وَتَسْتَخْرِجُونَ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَهَا ۖ وَتَرَى ٱلْفُلْكَ فِيهِ مَوَاخِرَ لِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ

The pattern of contrasts continues throughout this surah. Allah ﷻ has been establishing that opposing realities cannot be equal: this life and the next life are not the same, day and night are not the same, the stages of your development inside your mother's womb and outside are not the same. The spouses are not the same. Now Allah ﷻ presents another comparison: the two kinds of ocean are not the same.

The first body of water is described with beautiful, layered meanings. هَـٰذَا عَذْبٌ فُرَاتٌ - this one is fresh water. The word فُرَات water that is so sweet, so refreshing, that even when you're not thirsty, you want more. It's endless, it keeps coming, there's so much of it. Then Allah ﷻ adds سَآئِغٌ شَرَابُهُۥ - its drink goes down easy on the throat. The word سَائِغ captures that smooth feeling when something pleasant passes through your throat. Think about the opposite: when you're trying to swallow cough medicine or antibiotics, it hurts to take it down. You might even choke on it. But this water? It goes down so easily, so smoothly.

The contrast is stark: وَهَـٰذَا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌ - and this other water is salty and bitter, like seawater. Yet despite this fundamental difference between sweet and salt water, Allah ﷻ reveals something remarkable: وَمِن كُلٍّ تَأْكُلُونَ لَحْمًا طَرِيًّا - from both you consume fresh meat. Whether it's river creatures from freshwater or sea creatures from saltwater, you get marine life that you can eat.

وَتَسْتَخْرِجُونَ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَهَا - and from either side, sweet water or salt water, you pull out pearls and ornaments that you wear. The treasures of both bodies of water are available to you.

Then Allah ﷻ paints a vivid image: وَتَرَى ٱلْفُلْكَ فِيهِ مَوَاخِرَ - you see the ships tearing through the water. The word مَوَاخِرَ comes from the image of tearing cloth. When you rip fabric, there's a visible tear with threads sticking out on both sides - this is different from cutting cloth cleanly. Allah ﷻ is describing how a ship moves through the ocean, leaving a wake behind it that looks like torn fabric, with water displaced on either side.

Why does all this exist? لِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ - so that you can pursue His bounty and so that you may be grateful. Most of what we wear, the technology we enjoy, even the simple pens and pencils we use - they were probably shipped to us from across the Atlantic. They didn't come by plane; that would be too expensive. Cartons upon cartons travel by sea. You might think this doesn't affect you if you live inland, but the reality is that the entire world is now impacted by sea travel. Global trade connects us all to these vast bodies of water that Allah ﷻ has subdued for our benefit.

translation: He causes the night to enter the day, and He causes the day to enter the night and has subjected the sun and the moon - each running [its course] for a specified term. That is Allah, your Lord; to Him belongs sovereignty. And those whom you invoke other than Him do not possess [as much as] the membrane of a date seed
commentary:
يُولِجُ ٱلَّيْلَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِن قِطْمِيرٍ

Allah ﷻ continues establishing His complete authority: يُولِجُ ٱلَّيْلَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ - He makes the night encroach into the day and He makes the day enter into the night. This constant transition, this gradual increase and decrease of daylight throughout the year, is under His control.

وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ - and He subdued the sun and the moon. The word سَخَّرَ means He overpowered them, domesticated them, brought them under His complete control. كُلٌّ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى - each one flows and runs until an appointed deadline.

After presenting these signs of His power, Allah ﷻ declares: ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ - that is Allah, your Master, to Him alone belongs all dominion. What's profound here is that in different parts of the surah, you find different kinds of evidence for Allah ﷻ. The Quran doesn't necessarily offer purely logical philosophical arguments for God's existence. It works from the foundational knowledge - not assumption, but knowledge - that human beings already recognize Allah ﷻ before they even come to this earth. The urge to be grateful and the urge to seek out their Master is already pre-programmed in the nature of the human being, as Allah ﷻ says: فِطْرَتَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي فَطَرَ النَّاسَ عَلَيْهَا (the natural disposition Allah has created in mankind).

The Quran doesn't engage in "Does God exist or not?" debates. There's no conversation about proving God's existence because there's no genuine doubt about it. When doubt is mentioned in the Quran, it's about revelation: how can you doubt this message is from Allah? The word كَافِر itself - one who disbelieves - literally means someone who buries or covers something. It's not that they don't have the truth; they bury it inside themselves, like someone burying a seed in the ground.

In this surah, the evidence presented for Allah ﷻ being the Master isn't revelation making arguments - rather, revelation is pushing you to look around. Look at the ocean. Look at the sky. Look at the plants. Look at the two different kinds of water. Just sit there, observe, and think about it. That's what Allah ﷻ wants from you.

I once had a friend who studied philosophy extensively. During a flight, he decided to conduct an experiment with himself: "Today I'm going to board this plane as an atheist and see if I come out a believer." He changed seats, refusing to talk for the entire 3.5-hour flight, just staring out the window at the clouds and stars. When we landed, he said, "There's absolutely a God. You can't look at the clouds and not believe." This was someone with a master's degree in philosophy, familiar with all its complex arguments - and what brought him back to certainty was simply observing the clouds.

The ayah concludes by addressing those who worship others besides Allah وَٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِن قِطْمِيرٍ - those you call upon besides Him don't even own the thin membrane covering a date seed. In Arabic, when someone is completely bankrupt, they say "he doesn't even own the crack in the pit of a date." Dates are like the widget of classical Arabic economics - the smallest item of value. But even the pit of a date has some worth because you could plant it. Now imagine that pit has a thin membrane covering it - that's قِطْمِير, the most worthless thing imaginable. Your false gods don't even possess that much power.

translation: If you invoke them, they do not hear your supplication; and if they heard, they would not respond to you. And on the Day of Resurrection they will deny your association. And none can inform you like [one] Acquainted [with all matters]
commentary:
إِن تَدْعُوهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُوا۟ دُعَآءَكُمْ وَلَوْ سَمِعُوا۟ مَا ٱسْتَجَابُوا۟ لَكُمْ ۖ وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ يَكْفُرُونَ بِشِرْكِكُمْ ۚ وَلَا يُنَبِّئُكَ مِثْلُ خَبِيرٍ

The powerlessness of false gods is made even clearer: إِن تَدْعُوهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُوا۟ دُعَآءَكُمْ - if you call upon them, they won't even hear your calls. These idols, these false deities, cannot perceive your prayers.

وَلَوْ سَمِعُوا۟ مَا ٱسْتَجَابُوا۟ لَكُمْ - and even if they could somehow hear you - if you were worshiping cows or angels who have the ability to hear - they still wouldn't be able to respond to you at all. The word مَا ٱسْتَجَابُوا۟ emphasizes they wouldn't even try to respond. They couldn't attempt it even if they wanted to.

Then comes the devastating reality of Judgment Day: وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ يَكْفُرُونَ بِشِرْكِكُمْ - on the Day of Resurrection, they will deny and disown all the shirk you committed with them. The very objects of your worship will reject you.

وَلَا يُنَبِّئُكَ مِثْلُ خَبِيرٍ - and no one will inform you like the One who truly knows. Who can give you more accurate news about what's coming than Allah ﷻ Himself, the All-Knowing?

translation: O mankind, you are those in need of Allah, while Allah is the Free of need, the Praiseworthy
commentary:
۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ أَنتُمُ ٱلْفُقَرَآءُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ هُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ٱلْحَمِيدُ

After establishing that the false gods are bankrupt and powerless, Allah ﷻ turns to address humanity directly: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ - O people, listen up!

أَنتُمُ ٱلْفُقَرَآءُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ - you yourselves are completely bankrupt before Allah. You are in absolute, constant need of Him. The word فُقَرَاء suggests people whose backs are broken from the burdens they carry, who have nothing, who are utterly destitute. Your bankruptcy is constantly being bailed out by Allah ﷻ, moment by moment.

وَٱللَّهُ هُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ٱلْحَمِيدُ - and Allah, He alone is Al-Ghani, the One who needs absolutely nothing, and Al-Hamid, the One who is inherently praiseworthy, praised in and of Himself.

translation: If He wills, He can do away with you and bring forth a new creation
commentary:
إِن يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَأْتِ بِخَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ

This reality is emphasized further: إِن يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ - if He wants, He will make all of you disappear, He will annihilate you completely. وَيَأْتِ بِخَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ - and bring about an entirely new creation to replace you. You're not that precious. You're not indispensable to Him.

translation: And that is for Allah not difficult
commentary:
وَمَا ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ بِعَزِيزٍ

وَمَا ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ بِعَزِيزٍ - and that is not difficult for Allah at all. It's not a big deal for Him. It wouldn't be a tough decision. When عَزِيز comes in this context with the preposition بِ, it means something is not hard, not challenging, not a matter of difficulty whatsoever.

translation: And no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another. And if a heavily laden soul calls [another] to [carry some of] its load, nothing of it will be carried, even if he should be a close relative. You can only warn those who fear their Lord unseen and have established prayer. And whoever purifies himself only purifies himself for [the benefit of] his soul. And to Allah is the [final] destination
commentary:
وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۚ وَإِن تَدْعُ مُثْقَلَةٌ إِلَىٰ حِمْلِهَا لَا يُحْمَلْ مِنْهُ شَىْءٌ وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَىٰٓ ۗ إِنَّمَا تُنذِرُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ ۚ وَمَن تَزَكَّىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَتَزَكَّىٰ لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۚ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ

A fundamental principle of Divine justice is established: وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ - no one will carry the burden of another. This is describing the Day of Judgment, and to appreciate the weight of these words, imagine this scene: Your parents just arrived at the airport. Your father is holding two heavy suitcases. What do you naturally do? "Let me get that for you," and you put them in the trunk. You see your mother carrying something: "Mom, give me that." You want to carry their burden. Your child tries to carry the milk carton from the car, and you say, "Let me help you." This is our nature - we help those we love carry their loads.

But then Judgment Day arrives. وَإِن تَدْعُ مُثْقَلَةٌ إِلَىٰ حِمْلِهَا - someone buried under the load of their deeds, completely weighed down, calls out for help. "Can you help me, please? Could you help me carry this a little?" Their family member is standing right there - their son, their father, their cousin, their brother.

لَا يُحْمَلْ مِنْهُ شَىْءٌ وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَىٰٓ - none of it will be carried by anyone else, even if he was the closest of relatives. Can you imagine that scene? No matter how close the relationship was in this world, on that Day, no one will be able to help carry that burden. It's a terrifying picture.

The ayah then shifts to describe who can actually benefit from the Prophet's warning: إِنَّمَا تُنذِرُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلْغَيْبِ - you can only truly warn those who fear their Master in the Unseen, those who are genuinely fearful of their Lord even when nobody else can see them.

وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ - and they established prayer consistently. These are the people who will respond to your warning.

وَمَن تَزَكَّىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَتَزَكَّىٰ لِنَفْسِهِۦ - and whoever makes an effort to purify and cleanse himself, he only does so for his own benefit. Your good deeds are for you. Your purification is for you.

وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ - and to Allah alone is the final return. Everyone will return to Him.

## Ayahs 19-22

وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ وَٱلْبَصِيرُ

وَلَا ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتُ وَلَا ٱلنُّورُ

وَلَا ٱلظِّلُّ وَلَا ٱلْحَرُورُ

وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْأَحْيَآءُ وَلَا ٱلْأَمْوَٰتُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُسْمِعُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ بِمُسْمِعٍ مَّن فِى ٱلْقُبُورِ

Allah ﷻ presents a series of powerful contrasts, building on the pattern established throughout the surah. Earlier we learned that two kinds of oceans aren't the same, spouses aren't the same, night and day aren't the same, the sky and earth aren't the same. The false gods compared to Allah ﷻ cannot be the same. The righteous and the wicked cannot be the same.

Now the comparisons intensify:

وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ وَٱلْبَصِيرُ - the blind and the one who can see cannot be the same. One person sees guidance clearly; another is blind to it.

وَلَا ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتُ وَلَا ٱلنُّورُ - darkness - notice it's plural, ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتُ, all these layers of darkness - and the true light cannot be the same.

وَلَا ٱلظِّلُّ وَلَا ٱلْحَرُورُ - nor can you be in comfortable shade and be the same as someone who is out in the blazing sun being scorched by heat. The word حَرُور refers to intense, burning heat.

وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْأَحْيَآءُ وَلَا ٱلْأَمْوَٰتُ - and the living and the dead cannot be the same. The people who have taken in this message are like earth that receives water and comes to life, sprouting vegetation. They're alive. The people who refuse this message are like barren land - rain or no rain, it doesn't benefit them. They're like the dead.

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُسْمِعُ مَن يَشَآءُ - no doubt Allah makes whoever He wants to listen, to truly hear.

وَمَآ أَنتَ بِمُسْمِعٍ مَّن فِى ٱلْقُبُورِ - and you cannot make those in the graves listen. This carries a dual meaning: literally, you cannot make the dead in their graves hear you. But metaphorically, these people who reject revelation should be considered like people already in their graves. They're as good as dead. You can't make them listen if they've chosen to close their hearts.

translation: Not equal are the blind and the seeing
Nor are the darknesses and the light
Nor are the shade and the heat
And not equal are the living and the dead. Indeed, Allah causes to hear whom He wills, but you cannot make hear those in the graves
You, [O Muhammad], are not but a warner
commentary:
إِنْ أَنتَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ

إِنْ أَنتَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ - you are nothing but a warner. This is a shift in tone from the previous surah. Earlier, Allah ﷻ described the Prophet ﷺ as بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا - a giver of good news and a warner. Now the emphasis is solely on warning, highlighting the gravity of rejecting the message.

translation: Indeed, We have sent you with the truth as a bringer of good tidings and a warner. And there was no nation but that there had passed within it a warner
commentary:
إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا ۚ وَإِن مِّنْ أُمَّةٍ إِلَّا خَلَا فِيهَا نَذِيرٌ

The description expands again: إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا - We have indeed sent you with the truth, as a giver of good news and as a warner.

Then comes a universal principle: وَإِن مِّنْ أُمَّةٍ إِلَّا خَلَا فِيهَا نَذِيرٌ - there is not a single nation in existence except that a warner already passed through them. Every civilization, every people had someone come to warn them. This includes the African tribes, the Native American nations, the isolated peoples in Australia or South America, the Hindu civilization, the Buddhist civilization - there's not a single one that a messenger didn't come to. We learned in previous surahs that Allah ﷻ sent messengers to the cultural centers of civilizations, to أُمَّهَاتِ الْقُرَىٰ (the mother cities), the capitals where culture radiates outward.

translation: And if they deny you - then already have those before them denied. Their messengers came to them with clear proofs and written ordinances and with the enlightening Scripture
commentary:
وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَبِٱلزُّبُرِ وَبِٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْمُنِيرِ

The Prophet ﷺ is comforted with historical perspective: وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ - and if they call you a liar, فَقَدْ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ - those who came long before them did exactly the same thing. They called their messengers liars too.

جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ - messengers came to them with the clearest proofs, وَبِٱلزُّبُرِ - and with scriptures. The word زُبُر can refer to large pieces of metal with writing on them, but it's also famously connected to the Zabur given to Prophet Dawud (AS), which was mentioned in the previous surah. So this word should be fresh in the reader's mind.

وَبِٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْمُنِيرِ - and they were given the enlightening book, the illuminating scripture.

translation: Then I seized the ones who disbelieved, and how [terrible] was My reproach
commentary:
ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ

Despite receiving clear guidance, they rejected it. ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ - then I seized those who disbelieved. Allah ﷻ Himself took hold of them.

فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ - then how was My punishment? How was My terrible, unspeakable destruction? The word نَكِير suggests something so awful it cannot be adequately described.

translation: Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky, and We produce thereby fruits of varying colors? And in the mountains are tracts, white and red of varying shades and [some] extremely black
commentary:
أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦ ثَمَرَٰتٍ مُّخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَٰنُهَا ۚ وَمِنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ جُدَدٌۢ بِيضٌ وَحُمْرٌ مُّخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَٰنُهَا وَغَرَابِيبُ سُودٌ

After these warnings, Allah ﷻ returns to signs in creation: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً - haven't you seen that Allah is the One who sent down water from the sky?

فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦ ثَمَرَٰتٍ مُّخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَٰنُهَا - then We brought forth from it fruits of all different colors and textures. The variety is astounding - different shades, different hues, different patterns.

وَمِنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ جُدَدٌۢ بِيضٌ وَحُمْرٌ - and among the mountains, there are fresh streaks and layers - some white, some red. The word جُدَد something new, fresh. When the season changes and plants cover a mountain, it can look completely white with leaves and snow, like a new mountain you don't even recognize from before. There are also mountains of pure red rock, like the red rock formations in Nevada.

مُّخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَٰنُهَا - how different their colors and textures are!

وَغَرَابِيبُ سُودٌ - and there are mountains of intense black. The word غَرَابِيب refers to the deep black of a crow's feathers - black so pure and complete that you don't even see a hint of gray texture in the rock. That's how black some mountains are.

translation: And among people and moving creatures and grazing livestock are various colors similarly. Only those fear Allah, from among His servants, who have knowledge. Indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Forgiving
commentary:
وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَٱلدَّوَآبِّ وَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ مُخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَٰنُهُۥ كَذَٰلِكَ ۗ إِنَّمَا يَخْشَى ٱللَّهَ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ ٱلْعُلَمَـٰٓؤُا۟ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ غَفُورٌ

The observation of color and diversity continues: وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَٱلدَّوَآبِّ وَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ مُخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَٰنُهُۥ كَذَٰلِكَ - and of people, all kinds of animals, and even cattle, there are different colors just like this. Allah ﷻ did a magnificent paint job on all things. He colored human beings differently, animals differently. One cow has the pattern that looks like a logo, another looks yellow, another brown. Mountains have different colors. People have different colors. Allah ﷻ gave those textures and hues to all of them.

These ayahs carry profound implications that crush the foundations of racism. Allah ﷻ takes pride in all colors. Look at how varied people's colors are - just like the variety in mountain colors, just like the diversity in flower colors, in plants, in fruits. Colors are part of Allah's artistry, they're a gift from Him. You don't get to say this color is better than that color because they're all from Allah ﷻ. Just as كَذَٰلِكَ - they have different textures as well.

Then comes one of the most powerful statements in the Quran: إِنَّمَا يَخْشَى ٱللَّهَ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ ٱلْعُلَمَـٰٓؤُا۟ - the only ones who truly fear Allah from among His slaves are those who have knowledge. This is a profound statement that connects to what was discussed earlier about the nature of knowledge. When you have genuine knowledge of Allah ﷻ and you look at all of this nature, it leads to خَشْيَة - a deep, reverential fear and awe of Allah ﷻ, coupled with appreciation of His magnificence.

But here's the critical point: if you don't have the knowledge of Allah ﷻ, you can study botany, you can study geology, you can be an expert in mountains, you can be a professional mountain climber, you could have seen the most beautiful places on this planet - and it doesn't necessarily give you anything from Allah ﷻ. That initial فِطْرَة, that pre-programmed faith that Allah ﷻ placed in every human being, has to remain preserved somehow within you. When that foundation exists and you observe the world around you, it will spark genuine faith. You'll come to recognize there is a God.

What's philosophically interesting here is that Allah ﷻ told us to appreciate these wonders of creation, but the relationship we develop with Him through this observation is described as خَشْيَة - fear. You might expect the ayah to say "the one who praises Allah will be the one of knowledge" or "he appreciates Allah." But instead, Allah ﷻ said يَخْشَى - he fears Him. Why fear? Because true knowledge of Allah's power, His complete control over all creation, His ability to create such magnificent diversity, naturally produces a sense of awe and humility. You recognize His absolute authority, and that recognition manifests as reverential fear combined with love and hope.

The ayah concludes: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ غَفُورٌ - indeed Allah is Mighty, Forgiving. He is عَزِيز - all-powerful, overwhelming in His might, the One who created all this diversity with effortless ease. And He is غَفُور - constantly forgiving, always ready to pardon those who turn to Him. These two attributes together provide the complete picture: fear His power, but hope in His mercy.

translation: Indeed, those who recite the Book of Allah and establish prayer and spend [in His cause] out of what We have provided them, secretly and publicly, [can] expect a profit that will never perish
commentary:
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَتْلُونَ كِتَـٰبَ ٱللَّهِ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَأَنفَقُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً يَرْجُونَ تِجَـٰرَةً لَّن تَبُورَ

Having found their purpose, believers now act upon it. This ayah describes those who continuously recite the Book of Allah ﷻ. This is نُورٌ عَلَىٰ نُورٍ (light upon light)—the light of revelation building upon the light of intellect and observation from the previous ayah.

Notice the three actions mentioned: they recite Allah's Book, they establish prayer, and they spend from what We have provided them, secretly and openly. The sequence is significant. The previous surah taught us about people who are completely absorbed in this worldly life—they're already in a form of punishment because they see nothing beyond the material. In contrast, these believers who have discovered their purpose refuse to forget it, so they continuously spend for Allah's sake.

Think of it this way: when you make deposits in your bank account, you constantly check your balance. The more you deposit, the more frequently you monitor it. Similarly, when you keep depositing in "Allah's bank"—the bank of the Hereafter—you remain constantly aware of it. Right now you have a banking app on your phone to check your worldly balance. Consider your conscience as the app for checking your Hereafter balance. These believers deposit continuously because they know this is their actual retirement plan, their true destination.

The ayah beautifully structures these three practices: reading the Book of Allah ﷻ and establishing prayer both involve speech—you're reciting in both. Spending, however, is action. This reflects the principle stated elsewhere in the Quran: إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ ٱلْكَلِمُ ٱلطَّيِّبُ وَٱلْعَمَلُ ٱلصَّـٰلِحُ يَرْفَعُهُۥ (To Him ascends the good word, and the righteous deed lifts it). First comes the good word—reciting the Book, making salah—then comes the action that elevates it: spending.

Notice that spending secretly (سِرًّا) is mentioned before openly (عَلَانِيَةً). Secret charity is generally better for the individual, protecting against showing off. But public spending has its place too—when you've matured spiritually, giving publicly can encourage others to give, and you receive a share of their reward for inspiring them.

The ayah concludes by describing their motivation: يَرْجُونَ تِجَـٰرَةً لَّن تَبُورَ (they hope for a transaction that will never decline). تَبُورَ means to go down until it's completely gone, to fail as an investment. They're engaged in a business venture that cannot possibly fail.

translation: That He may give them in full their rewards and increase for them of His bounty. Indeed, He is Forgiving and Appreciative
commentary:
لِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أُجُورَهُمْ وَيَزِيدَهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ غَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ

This profitable transaction will result in Allah ﷻ compensating them fully for their works—He will give them their complete payment—وَيَزِيدَهُم (and increase them) مِّن فَضْلِهِۦٓ (from His bounty). Not only will they receive exactly what they earned, but Allah ﷻ will add to it from His generosity.

The ayah concludes with two divine attributes: إِنَّهُۥ غَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ (Indeed, He is Forgiving and Appreciative). We've seen غَفُورٌ (Ghafoor) before, but now we learn these behaviors earn forgiveness. But Allah ﷻ adds another beautiful quality: شَكُورٌ (Shakoor).

Understanding Shakoor requires explanation. Some divine names are shared between Allah ﷻ and His servants, like تَوَّابٌ. We describe a slave as تَائِبٌ (one who turns back to Allah), and we describe Allah ﷻ as تَوَّابٌ (One who turns back to us, accepts our repentance). The same relationship exists with Shakoor. We are شَاكِرٌ (grateful ones), and Allah ﷻ is شَكُورٌ (the Appreciative One).

For us, شُكْرٌ (gratitude) means being extremely grateful, always thinking of His blessings and acknowledging them abundantly. Elsewhere in the Quran, Allah ﷻ says وَقَلِيلٌ مِّنْ عِبَادِىَ ٱلشَّكُورُ (And few of My servants are truly grateful). But when Allah ﷻ is described as Shakoor, it means He is extremely appreciative of what His slaves do—He acknowledges and values their efforts.

In English, the word "appreciation" doesn't fully capture this. There's artificial appreciation—like a generic thank-you email after an event—empty words without genuine recognition. But then there's when your employer calls you into the office and says, "You put in so many hours. I can't begin to thank you. I'm so appreciative of what you did. You made me extremely proud." That's true appreciation, genuine acknowledgment of effort.

Allah ﷻ, as Shakoor, genuinely appreciates the deeds you and I do. He forgives our shortcomings, and He is extremely appreciative of the effort we make. What profound mercy—that the Creator of the universe values our small acts and acknowledges them with such generosity.

translation: And that which We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], of the Book is the truth, confirming what was before it. Indeed, Allah, of His servants, is Acquainted and Seeing
commentary:
وَٱلَّذِىٓ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ مِنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِعِبَادِهِۦ لَخَبِيرٌۢ بَصِيرٌ

The pattern described in the previous ayahs—people reading the Book, establishing prayer, and giving charity—has always existed among believers throughout history. This wasn't exclusive to the Muslims after the Prophet ﷺ. Previous believing communities also read their scriptures, prayed, and gave charity. But now comes the pinnacle, the finale: what better book could you possibly read? What greater revelation could you receive?

وَٱلَّذِىٓ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ مِنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ (That which We have revealed to you from the Book) هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ (it is the Truth) مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ (confirming what came before it)—confirming the Torah and other previous scriptures that came right before it in the timeline of revelation.

The ayah concludes: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِعِبَادِهِۦ لَخَبِيرٌۢ بَصِيرٌ (Indeed, Allah is, concerning His servants, Acquainted and Seeing). He has complete information about His slaves—He is fully informed—and He has complete vision of them. He knows who deserves to receive this final revelation and who will benefit from it.

translation: Then we caused to inherit the Book those We have chosen of Our servants; and among them is he who wrongs himself, and among them is he who is moderate, and among them is he who is foremost in good deeds by permission of Allah. That [inheritance] is what is the great bounty
commentary:
ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱصْطَفَيْنَا مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ۖ فَمِنْهُمْ ظَالِمٌ لِّنَفْسِهِۦ وَمِنْهُم مُّقْتَصِدٌ وَمِنْهُمْ سَابِقٌۢ بِٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَضْلُ ٱلْكَبِيرُ

ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ (Then We gave the Book as inheritance) ٱلَّذِينَ ٱصْطَفَيْنَا مِنْ عِبَادِنَا (to those We chose from among Our servants). This is remarkable. The word اصْطَفَيْنَا (We chose) typically describes prophets—divine selection. Here, Allah ﷻ says even you, even ordinary members of this Ummah, being chosen to receive and carry this Book is a divine selection.

Notice Allah ﷻ combines two concepts: أَوْرَثْنَا (inheritance) and اصْطَفَيْنَا (selection). Normally, inheritance isn't a choice—whoever is in your lineage gets the inheritance automatically. But Allah ﷻ combines both: yes, the Book is inherited from one generation to the next, passed down through time, but even then there's a special divine choice involved. There are people who don't deserve to receive this Book even if they're born into a generation of Muslims, and there are people who deserve to receive it even if they're not born into a Muslim generation.

Among those who inherit the Book, Allah ﷻ describes three categories:

فَمِنْهُمْ ظَالِمٌ لِّنَفْسِهِۦ (Among them is he who wrongs himself). This person has the Book, understands it, reads it, listens to it, studies it—yet still does wrong things. He's a Muslim who fails to live up to the message.

وَمِنْهُم مُّقْتَصِدٌ (And among them is he who is moderate). مُقْتَصِدٌ is the one who tries to be fair to the text. He's not terrible, not excellent—he's in the middle. He passes with a satisfactory grade, just surviving.

Notice the order: Allah ﷻ mentions the one who fails first, then the one in the middle. Only then: وَمِنْهُمْ سَابِقٌۢ بِٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ (And among them is he who is foremost in good deeds by permission of Allah). This is the honor roll student, the one who competes and races in good deeds. Why? Because this person understands that when revelation came, it gave access to treasures that were previously impossible to obtain. The real treasure is earning good deeds, so this person competes to accumulate them.

بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ (By Allah's permission)—even this racing toward good is by divine grace and permission, not purely personal achievement.

ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَضْلُ ٱلْكَبِيرُ (That is the great bounty). What is "that"? To be among those who compete in good deeds—that itself is the ultimate gift, the ultimate grant from Allah ﷻ.

translation: [For them are] gardens of perpetual residence which they will enter. They will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their garments therein will be silk
commentary:
جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَهَا يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيهَا مِنْ أَسَاوِرَ مِن ذَهَبٍ وَلُؤْلُؤًا ۖ وَلِبَاسُهُمْ فِيهَا حَرِيرٌ

For those who compete in good deeds, their reward awaits: جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَهَا (Gardens of perpetual residence they will enter). يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيهَا مِنْ أَسَاوِرَ مِن ذَهَبٍ وَلُؤْلُؤًا (They will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and pearls). They'll be decorated, decked out with ornaments of the finest materials.

وَلِبَاسُهُمْ فِيهَا حَرِيرٌ (And their garments therein will be silk). حَرِيرٌ refers to an exotic, thick form of silk—the finest clothing imaginable, their standard attire in Paradise.

translation: And they will say, "Praise to Allah, who has removed from us [all] sorrow. Indeed, our Lord is Forgiving and Appreciative
commentary:
وَقَالُوا۟ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَذْهَبَ عَنَّا ٱلْحَزَنَ ۖ إِنَّ رَبَّنَا لَغَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ

وَقَالُوا۟ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ (And they will say, "All praise belongs to Allah"). Notice how this connects back to where the story began—with الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ, all praise and gratitude belonging to Allah ﷻ.

ٱلَّذِىٓ أَذْهَبَ عَنَّا ٱلْحَزَنَ (Who has removed from us all grief). حَزَن encompasses all forms of sadness, worry, anxiety, and grief. Paradise is where your grief is removed completely. This carries a powerful implication for believers in this world: be ready for a life that includes grief here. It's acceptable, even expected, because in Jannah you'll witness Allah ﷻ removing all grief permanently.

إِنَّ رَبَّنَا لَغَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ (Indeed, our Lord is Forgiving and Appreciative). These people now say these divine names because they've experienced them in action. In Paradise, they witness the full manifestation of Allah's forgiveness and appreciation. What was promise in this world is now reality they're living.

translation: He who has settled us in the home of duration out of His bounty. There touches us not in it any fatigue, and there touches us not in it weariness [of mind]
commentary:
ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحَلَّنَا دَارَ ٱلْمُقَامَةِ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ لَا يَمَسُّنَا فِيهَا نَصَبٌ وَلَا يَمَسُّنَا فِيهَا لُغُوبٌ

ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحَلَّنَا دَارَ ٱلْمُقَامَةِ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ (Who has settled us in the Home of Duration out of His bounty). دَارَ ٱلْمُقَامَةِ—the home of permanent residence. The word مُقَامَة is powerful here; it comes from the verb قَامَ يَقُومُ (to stand, to remain), but with the additional form that emphasizes permanence and continuation. This is where we stay forever.

مِن فَضْلِهِۦ (Out of His bounty)—not because we earned it solely through our deeds, but from His generous favor.

لَا يَمَسُّنَا فِيهَا نَصَبٌ (Not touching us therein is any fatigue). نَصَب means exhaustion so severe you cannot stand up anymore—complete physical depletion.

وَلَا يَمَسُّنَا فِيهَا لُغُوبٌ (And not touching us therein is weariness). لُغُوبٌ means being crushed under too much work, being overworked to the point of breaking.

Reading between the lines, we understand what these people experienced in worldly life. They stood before Allah ﷻ in prayer until they couldn't stand anymore. They worked for Allah ﷻ until they were exhausted. They exhausted themselves serving Him. Now in Paradise, they're grateful because those difficult days of exhaustion are over. The period of being overworked has ended—now they're on permanent vacation.

This necessarily implies what this life should be: this is the life to be overworked, to engage yourself fully in good deeds, to exhaust yourself in service to Allah ﷻ. The rest comes later.

translation: And for those who disbelieve will be the fire of Hell. [Death] is not decreed for them so they may die, nor will its torment be lightened for them. Thus do we recompense every ungrateful one
commentary:
وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَهُمْ نَارُ جَهَنَّمَ لَا يُقْضَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ فَيَمُوتُوا۟ وَلَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُم مِّنْ عَذَابِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى كُلَّ كَفُورٍ

In stark contrast, وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَهُمْ نَارُ جَهَنَّمَ (those who disbelieved will have the Fire of Hell). لَا يُقْضَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ فَيَمُوتُوا۟ (It will not be decreed for them that they die). The punishment won't finish them off; it won't kill them so they can find relief in death.

وَلَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُم مِّنْ عَذَابِهَا (Nor will its torment be lightened for them). It's not that the blows aren't lethal enough—the punishment simply won't be reduced at all.

كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى كُلَّ كَفُورٍ (Thus do We recompense every ungrateful one). كَفُورٍ—the extremely ungrateful, those who persistently rejected and denied the truth.

translation: And they will cry out therein, "Our Lord, remove us; we will do righteousness - other than what we were doing!" But did We not grant you life enough for whoever would remember therein to remember, and the warner had come to you? So taste [the punishment], for there is not for the wrongdoers any helper
commentary:
وَهُمْ يَصْطَرِخُونَ فِيهَا رَبَّنَآ أَخْرِجْنَا نَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا غَيْرَ ٱلَّذِى كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ ۚ أَوَلَمْ نُعَمِّرْكُم مَّا يَتَذَكَّرُ فِيهِ مَن تَذَكَّرَ وَجَآءَكُمُ ٱلنَّذِيرُ ۖ فَذُوقُوا۟ فَمَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مِن نَّصِيرٍ

وَهُمْ يَصْطَرِخُونَ فِيهَا (And they will cry out therein). يَصْطَرِخُونَ means crying out for help as loudly as they possibly can, screaming desperately.

رَبَّنَآ أَخْرِجْنَا نَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا غَيْرَ ٱلَّذِى كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ ("Our Lord, remove us; we will do righteousness—other than what we were doing!"). Everything we did before, we promise not to do again. We'll do the exact opposite.

The response comes: أَوَلَمْ نُعَمِّرْكُم مَّا يَتَذَكَّرُ فِيهِ مَن تَذَكَّرَ (Did We not grant you life enough for whoever would be reminded therein to be reminded?). Didn't We give you a long enough life? You had sufficient time—an age at which anyone who wanted to take heed and benefit from the reminder could have done so. Are you claiming you didn't get enough time? That you didn't receive adequate warning? That I gave you less time than you deserved?

وَجَآءَكُمُ ٱلنَّذِيرُ (And the warner came to you). On top of that, the warner—the Prophet ﷺ—came to deliver the message clearly.

فَذُوقُوا۟ (So taste). Just taste what you deserve.

فَمَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مِن نَّصِيرٍ (For the wrongdoers there is not any helper). No one will come to your aid whatsoever.

translation: Indeed, Allah is Knower of the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and earth. Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts
commentary:
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَـٰلِمُ غَيْبِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ

This ayah comes immediately after those in Hell claim they need more time and would change their behavior. Allah ﷻ responds: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَـٰلِمُ غَيْبِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ (Indeed, Allah is Knower of the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and the earth). He is the only One who truly knows the غَيْب (unseen) of the skies and the earth.

إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts). He knows what's going on deep inside you—your hidden thoughts, concealed intentions, secret motivations.

The connection is clear: Allah ﷻ is saying, "I know things you cannot even see. I know your intentions and your true nature. When you claim you would change your behavior if given another chance, I know your reality. You are exactly the type of person who, if I sent you back, would return to your old ways. I'm not guessing—I know. When I let you remain in Hell, it's because you absolutely deserve it based on who you truly are."

translation: It is He who has made you successors upon the earth. And whoever disbelieves - upon him will be [the consequence of] his disbelief. And the disbelief of the disbelievers does not increase them in the sight of their Lord except in hatred; and the disbelief of the disbelievers does not increase them except in loss
commentary:
هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَكُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ فَمَن كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُۥ ۖ وَلَا يَزِيدُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا مَقْتًا ۖ وَلَا يَزِيدُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ إِلَّا خَسَارًا

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَكُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ (It is He who has made you successors upon the earth). خَلَـٰٓئِفَ means those who are left behind—generation after generation inheriting the earth, each replacing the one before.

فَمَن كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُۥ (So whoever disbelieves—upon him is [the consequence of] his disbelief). The burden of disbelief falls entirely on the one who chooses it. The liability is his alone.

وَلَا يَزِيدُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا مَقْتًا (And the disbelief of the disbelievers does not increase them in the sight of their Lord except in hatred). This works in two directions. First, as disbelievers continue and deepen their rejection, they become more disgusted with Allah ﷻ Himself. They don't want to hear about Him. They become more aggressive and agitated the deeper they go into disbelief. Second, simultaneously, the more they immerse themselves in disbelief, Allah ﷻ becomes that much more disgusted with them.

مَقْتًا is a significant word. Historically, مَقْت referred to a disgusting tribal practice where, after a father died, his son would marry his father's widows (his stepmothers), claiming they were women of the tribe. This abhorrently repulsive practice became known as مَقْت—something absolutely intolerable, so disgusting you're disturbed even hearing about it. Allah ﷻ uses this word to describe the mutual disgust between Him and persistent disbelievers.

وَلَا يَزِيدُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ إِلَّا خَسَارًا (And the disbelief of the disbelievers does not increase them except in loss). Beyond the disgust, their disbelief brings them nothing but continuous loss in every dimension.

translation: Say, "Have you considered your 'partners' whom you invoke besides Allah? Show me what they have created from the earth, or have they partnership [with Him] in the heavens? Or have We given them a book so they are [standing] on evidence therefrom? [No], rather, the wrongdoers do not promise each other except delusion
commentary:
قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ شُرَكَآءَكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَرُونِى مَاذَا خَلَقُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ أَمْ لَهُمْ شِرْكٌ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ أَمْ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ كِتَـٰبًا فَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَتٍ مِّنْهُ ۚ بَلْ إِن يَعِدُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ بَعْضُهُم بَعْضًا إِلَّا غُرُورًا

قُلْ (Say): The Prophet ﷺ is instructed to challenge the disbelievers directly.

أَرَءَيْتُمْ شُرَكَآءَكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ (Have you considered your 'partners' whom you invoke besides Allah?). These idols represent something—deities, concepts, powers. Have you actually examined what these representations are?

أَرُونِى مَاذَا خَلَقُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ (Show me what they have created of the earth). Point to something concrete they made.

أَمْ لَهُمْ شِرْكٌ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ (Or do they have partnership in [creation of] the heavens?). Do they have any share, any role in creating or maintaining the heavens?

أَمْ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ كِتَـٰبًا فَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَتٍ مِّنْهُ (Or have We given them a book so they are [standing] on evidence therefrom?). Did these idol-worshippers receive some revelation, some book that gives them clear proof that their worship is legitimate?

The answer to all these questions is obviously no. So what's really happening?

بَلْ إِن يَعِدُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ بَعْضُهُم بَعْضًا إِلَّا غُرُورًا (Rather, the wrongdoers do not promise each other except delusion). This exposes the reality: it's groupthink, gang mentality. They promise each other nothing but deception. They encourage one another: "We must hold on. We must stay true to our traditions, our norms, our country, our patriotism, our religion. How can we abandon our religion? It's our legacy, our honor." They rally each other to remain off-track, but it's all based on غُرُورًا—delusion and deception.

translation: Indeed, Allah holds the heavens and the earth, lest they cease. And if they should cease, no one could hold them [in place] after Him. Indeed, He is Forbearing and Forgiving
commentary:
۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُمْسِكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ أَن تَزُولَا ۚ وَلَئِن زَالَتَآ إِنْ أَمْسَكَهُمَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِّنۢ بَعْدِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ حَلِيمًا غَفُورًا

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُمْسِكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ أَن تَزُولَا (Indeed, Allah holds the heavens and the earth, lest they cease). يُمْسِكُ means to hold, to keep in place. تَزُولَا means to fall apart, to cease existing, to disintegrate.

وَلَئِن زَالَتَآ إِنْ أَمْسَكَهُمَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِّنۢ بَعْدِهِۦٓ (And if they should cease, no one could hold them after Him). If the heavens and earth were to fall apart, no one—absolutely no one—would be able to hold them together again besides Him.

The implication is staggering: the crimes of human beings are severe enough that the heavens and earth should rightfully fall apart because of them. But it's Allah's forbearance, His حِلْم, and His extreme forgiveness that keeps the skies and earth in place despite humanity's transgressions.

إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ حَلِيمًا غَفُورًا (Indeed, He is Forbearing and Forgiving). His حِلْم (forbearance)—His patience with human evil—and His غُفْران (forgiveness) are what prevent immediate cosmic destruction.

translation: And they swore by Allah their strongest oaths that if a warner came to them, they would be more guided than [any] one of the [previous] nations. But when a warner came to them, it did not increase them except in aversion
commentary:
وَأَقْسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ لَئِن جَآءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ لَّيَكُونُنَّ أَهْدَىٰ مِنْ إِحْدَى ٱلْأُمَمِ ۖ فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ مَّا زَادَهُمْ إِلَّا نُفُورًا

وَأَقْسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ (And they swore by Allah their strongest oaths). جَهْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ means they made the most emphatic, emotional oaths, putting maximum effort into their swearing.

This describes political theater—leaders of Quraysh would gather the public, stand with the Ka'bah behind them, and make dramatic declarations: "We swear to Allah, if in fact a warner comes to us, we would be the first to accept!"

لَئِن جَآءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ لَّيَكُونُنَّ أَهْدَىٰ مِنْ إِحْدَى ٱلْأُمَمِ (That if a warner came to them, they would be more guided than [any] one of the [previous] nations). "We would accept more completely than any nation in history!" It was an impressive speech. The crowd would respond enthusiastically: "Yes, our leader is right! If there was a real messenger, we'd definitely follow him. But Muhammad ﷺ isn't a messenger." They would rile up the crowd with emotional rhetoric, and people would think, "That was an awesome speech. If he says Muhammad isn't a messenger, then he must not be."

فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ مَّا زَادَهُمْ إِلَّا نُفُورًا (But when a warner came to them, it did not increase them except in aversion). When the warner actually came, their response was the opposite—it increased them in nothing but نُفُورًا (extreme hatred and aversion).

نُفُورًا comes from نَفَرَ—a military battalion going out on attack, a small squad on a mission to eliminate someone. The word captures mob hatred, the kind that turns into violent pursuit. When the Messenger ﷺ came, instead of accepting him, they became a hateful mob intent on destroying him.

translation: [Due to] arrogance in the land and plotting of evil; but the evil plot does not encompass except its own people. Then do they await except the way of the former peoples? But you will never find in the way of Allah any change, and you will never find in the way of Allah any alteration
commentary:
ٱسْتِكْبَارًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَكْرَ ٱلسَّيِّئِ ۚ وَلَا يَحِيقُ ٱلْمَكْرُ ٱلسَّيِّئُ إِلَّا بِأَهْلِهِۦ ۚ فَهَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا سُنَّتَ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ ۚ فَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ ٱللَّهِ تَبْدِيلًا ۖ وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ ٱللَّهِ تَحْوِيلًا

ٱسْتِكْبَارًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ([Due to] arrogance in the land). Their rejection stemmed from استكبار—seeking to maintain their arrogance, their sense of superiority and greatness in their city.

وَمَكْرَ ٱلسَّيِّئِ (And the evil plot). They plotted evil schemes against the truth and the Messenger ﷺ.

وَلَا يَحِيقُ ٱلْمَكْرُ ٱلسَّيِّئُ إِلَّا بِأَهْلِهِۦ (But the evil plot does not encompass except its own people). This is the Quran's version of "what goes around comes around." يَحِيقُ means to come back and attack, to encircle and strike. The evil plot doesn't return to strike anyone except its own architect, the one who designed it.

فَهَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا سُنَّتَ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (Then do they await except the way of the former peoples?). Are they waiting for the same pattern that befell previous nations who rejected their messengers?

فَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ ٱللَّهِ تَبْدِيلًا (But you will not find in the way of Allah any change). تَبْدِيلًا means alteration, substitution—Allah's established pattern will not be changed.

وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ ٱللَّهِ تَحْوِيلًا (And you will not find in the way of Allah any alteration). تَحْوِيلًا means amendment, slight modification—you won't find even minor adjustments to how Allah ﷻ deals with those who reject His messengers.

translation: Have they not traveled through the land and observed how was the end of those before them? And they were greater than them in power. But Allah is not to be caused failure by anything in the heavens or on the earth. Indeed, He is ever Knowing and Competent
commentary:
أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ وَكَانُوٓا۟ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً ۚ وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيُعْجِزَهُۥ مِن شَىْءٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَلَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَلِيمًا قَدِيرًا

أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ (Have they not traveled through the land and observed how was the end of those before them?). Haven't they journeyed and seen the ruins, the archaeological evidence of destroyed civilizations?

وَكَانُوٓا۟ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً (And they were greater than them in power). This thread runs through many Makkan surahs: those previous nations were far more powerful than the Quraysh. The Quraysh don't possess even a tenth of what those civilizations had.

وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيُعْجِزَهُۥ مِن شَىْءٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَلَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ (And Allah is not to be caused failure by anything in the heavens or on the earth). يُعْجِزَ means to overpower, to make incapable. Nothing in creation—in the heavens or earth—has ever been able to overpower Allah ﷻ or make Him incapable.

The previous surah mentioned armies trying to overpower Allah's will through their military might. This ayah clarifies: no creation will ever overpower Him.

إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَلِيمًا قَدِيرًا (Indeed, He has ever been Knowing and Competent). عَلِيمًا—fully knowledgeable, aware of everything. قَدِيرًا—fully capable, possessing complete calculation and power over all things.

translation: And if Allah were to impose blame on the people for what they have earned, He would not leave upon the earth any creature. But He defers them for a specified term. And when their time comes, then indeed Allah has ever been, of His servants, Seeing
commentary:
وَلَوْ يُؤَاخِذُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلنَّاسَ بِمَا كَسَبُوا۟ مَا تَرَكَ عَلَىٰ ظَهْرِهَا مِن دَآبَّةٍ وَلَـٰكِن يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۖ فَإِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِۦ بَصِيرًۢا

وَلَوْ يُؤَاخِذُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلنَّاسَ بِمَا كَسَبُوا۟ (And if Allah were to impose blame on the people for what they have earned). يُؤَاخِذُ means to seize, to grab hold of with accountability. If Allah ﷻ were to seize humanity immediately for what they've earned through their sins...

مَا تَرَكَ عَلَىٰ ظَهْرِهَا مِن دَآبَّةٍ (He would not leave upon it [i.e., the earth] any creature). دَآبَّة means any living creature—human, animal, insect, sea creature, microorganism. Not a single living thing would remain.

The magnitude of this statement is overwhelming. Human beings have committed so much sin on this planet that if Allah ﷻ gave them what they immediately deserved, life itself would cease to exist. The destruction would be so violent, so comprehensive, that no creature at the bottom of the ocean would survive. No amoeba, no ant—nothing would remain alive on earth. And this was already true by the time the Quran was revealed. We're not even discussing modern times—this was the state of humanity's sin over 1,400 years ago.

وَلَـٰكِن يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى (But He defers them for a specified term). However, He keeps postponing, delaying, putting them off إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى—until a named, fixed deadline.

The phrasing يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى implies continuous postponement. The punishment is deserved now, and now, and now. Everything in creation turns to Allah ﷻ asking, "Can we tear apart now? They deserve it." And Allah ﷻ says, "No." Then again, "Can we now?" "No." Again and again—He tells them to hold back until the Day of Judgment. Everything in the skies is about to rupture because of humanity's sins, but the only thing holding them together, the only thing saying "Hold on, hold on, not yet" is Allah ﷻ Himself.

فَإِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهُمْ (But when their term has come...). When that deadline finally arrives, when He finally gives the signal...

Notice what Allah ﷻ doesn't say. He doesn't describe how the earth will shatter, the oceans will boil over, the sun will crash into the moon. He doesn't detail the cosmic destruction. Instead, He says only:

فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِۦ بَصِيرًۢا (Then indeed, Allah has ever been, of His servants, Seeing).

This understated conclusion carries immense weight. It's enough for you to know that your sins until now were sufficient to tear the skies apart. Allah ﷻ has been in full view—بَصِيرًۢا—of all His slaves, every single deed of every single human being throughout history. Now imagine: the earthquake of Judgment Day will be compensation proportionate to all those deeds. Consider how serious that earthquake will be when Allah ﷻ has kept full view of every action of every person who ever lived.

بَصِيرًۢا encompasses both the external and internal—whatever we've done outwardly and whatever intentions we've harbored inwardly. Whatever motives and drives we've hidden, whatever loves and ambitions we've carried, whatever temptations and desires we've concealed—Allah ﷻ has been in full view of all of it. He knows which internal struggles you kept inside and transformed into good words and righteous deeds that ascended to Him. He knows which ones manifested as evil. All of it—Allah ﷻ sees.

May Allah ﷻ make us among those who are safe on that terrifying day.
